Accept and clear Firecracker's default None policy, retain V1N1 as replaceable GET-visible pending configuration, and reject execution before backend construction because Apple Silicon cannot satisfy its documented Neoverse V1 source contract; x86 static names remain foreign policies.

alternatives: Applying only Firecracker's four V1N1 masks cannot establish its complete unmasked Neoverse N1 identity on an unrelated Apple source model.
Inventing an Apple-to-N1 feature lattice or fingerprint would lack an authoritative pinned source and create a new portability contract.
Treating successful HVF writes and readback as proof of the documented Neoverse V1 source precondition confuses mechanical writability with CPU-model compatibility.

capabilities.summary: Accept Firecracker's default None page policy and return a stable pre-allocation platform fault for exact Linux hugetlbfs-backed 2M after matching its even-MiB rule; public arm64 macOS/XNU/HVF has no exact backing equivalent.

alternatives: 16-KiB host pages or HVF IPA granules affect different translation layers and are not 2-MiB hugetlbfs backing.
2-MiB virtual alignment or batched map/protect operations preserve ordinary host backing and change the observable contract.
A Linux VM or sidecar would back a different process and abandon bangbang's native Apple-Silicon/HVF product boundary.
Private memory APIs are unsupported and violate the repository's public-API and production-signing boundary.
Root-only host changes would add a privileged deployment and security contract absent from the accepted target.
